background: A case-control study was conducted to investigate the aetiology of acoustic neuromas. Men aged 25-69 residing in Los Angeles County were eligible for inclusion. A total of 118 men were identified, but 28 were not interviewed due to various reasons. For 86 remaining patients, a neighborhood control of the same race and within five years of age was identified and interviewed. Both cases and controls were interviewed in the same manner to collect information about life experiences, with a focus on exposure to loud noise at work. Overall, 58 cases and 46 controls reported exposure to loud noise at work. Among the case-control pairs, 20 pairs had cases exposed but not controls, and 8 pairs had controls exposed but not cases.

task.query: Carry out an appropriate analysis to compare the proportions of exposed cases and controls.
task.answer:
From the information given, a 2x2 table can be constructed as follows:

|           | Cases (+) | Cases (-) | Total |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|-------|
| Controls (+) | 38        | 8         | 46    |
| Controls (-) | 20        | 20        | 40    |
| Total     | 58        | 28        | 86    |

The proportions of cases and controls reporting exposure to loud noise were 0.674 (58/86) and 0.535 (46/86), respectively. The difference in proportions is 0.14, with the 95% CI of 0.02 to 0.26. The proportions can be compared using McNemar's test, which gives:

zc = 11 / sqrt(28) = 2.08 (P = 0.04).

task.query: Calculate the odds ratio for acoustic neuroma associated with exposure to loud noise at work.
task.answer: The odds ratio is estimated as 20 / 8 = 2.5. The 95% CI (method not given in Chapter 10) is 1.05 to 6.56.
